content: UC Berkeley distinguishes between the Bachelor of Arts in Computer Science and the Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences. The BA path is housed in the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society and combines university, campus, college, and major requirements.

The Berkeley BA in Computer Science includes lower-division preparation built around COMPSCI 61A, COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL, COMPSCI 70, and COMPSCI 61C, plus approved mathematics support. The plan of study shows five upper-division CS major courses and one approved upper-division non-CS technical elective.

The BA major page highlights an upper-division design-course bucket that includes COMPSCI 152, 160, 161, 162, 164, and 168, and the sample schedule labels the upper-division CS major sequence as five major courses. Students also complete one approved upper-division non-CS technical elective.

The BA path requires the university and campus requirements plus the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society requirements. The page states that CDSS breadth is the same as the College of Letters and Science Seven-Course Breadth requirement, except that foreign language may be used to satisfy International Studies. The page also notes the bachelor's degree minimum of 120 units and at least 36 upper-division units.

The Computer Science minor consists of seven courses: COMPSCI 61A, COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL, COMPSCI 61C, COMPSCI 70, and three upper-division COMPSCI courses. The page states that at least three upper-division courses for the minor must be taken at Berkeley and that students must earn at least a 2.0 GPA in the upper-division courses used for the minor.

program: BS Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences
content: The Berkeley BS in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences is housed in the College of Engineering. The major page emphasizes engineering-science breadth plus a design experience selected from designated EECS and COMPSCI courses such as COMPSCI 160, 161, 162, 164, 169A with 169L, and 186.

The sample Berkeley BA plan sequences lower-division CS early and places upper-division design and theory courses after COMPSCI 61C and COMPSCI 70. It also shows that breadth and campus requirements continue alongside major courses rather than being fully separated from them.
